title: ranunculus philonotis subsp. trilobus
intro: Ranunculus philonotis subsp. trilobus, also known as Trilobed Ranunculus, is a plant species in the buttercup family that is native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. In herbal medicine, Ranunculus philonotis subsp. trilobus is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment to support skin health and promote wound recovery.
